heroPoems.title: The Soft Overcomes
heroPoems.firstStanza:
Nothing is softer than water.
Nothing is stronger
at wearing down stone.

heroPoems.title: O brother, my heart yearns for that true Guru, who fills the cup
heroPoems.firstStanza:
O brother, my heart yearns for that true Guru, who fills the cup
of true love, and drinks of it himself, and offers it then to
me.
He removes the veil from the eyes, and gives the true Vision of
Brahma:
He reveals the worlds in Him, and makes me to hear the Unstruck

heroPoems.title: The Aim Was Song
heroPoems.firstStanza:
Before man came to blow it right
The wind once blew itself untaught,
And did its loudest day and night
In any rough place where it caught.
Man came to tell it what was wrong:
It hadn’t found the place to blow;
heroPoems.tagline: For rooms lit by one lamp.

heroPoems.title: Morning at the Window
heroPoems.firstStanza:
They are rattling breakfast plates in basement kitchens,
And along the trampled edges of the street
I am aware of the damp souls of housemaids
Sprouting despondently at area gates.
The brown waves of fog toss up to me
Twisted faces from the bottom of the street,

heroPoems.title: Ode to a Nightingale
heroPoems.firstStanza:
My heart aches, and a drowsy numbness pains
My sense, as though of hemlock I had drunk,
Or emptied some dull opiate to the drains
One minute past, and Lethe-wards had sunk:
'Tis not through envy of thy happy lot,
But being too happy in thine happiness,—

heroPoems.title: Smoke in Every Fire
heroPoems.firstStanza:
Every duty carries flaws—
do it anyway,
cleanly.
heroPoems.tagline: For slow hours with a warm cup.

heroPoems.title: When I was young
heroPoems.firstStanza:
When I was young
I put books aside
and wanted to be a saint.
I wandered many springs as a beggar monk.

heroPoems.title: Naive heart, what has happened to you?
heroPoems.firstStanza:
Naive heart, what has happened to you?
What is this pain that has no diagnosis?

heroPoems.title: When the heart is hard and parched up
heroPoems.firstStanza:
When the heart is hard and parched up, come upon me with a shower of
mercy.
When grace is lost from life, come with a burst of song.
When tumultuous work raises its din on all sides shutting me out from
beyond, come to me, my lord of silence, with thy peace and rest.
When my beggarly heart sits crouched, shut up in a corner, break open
heroPoems.tagline: For when the street goes quiet.

heroPoems.title: Pour the cup again I thought love would be easy
heroPoems.firstStanza:
Pour the cup again I thought love would be easy
but every step has turned to steep stone
I asked the wind for the scent of her hair
it returned with blood-warm tears
heroPoems.tagline: For rooms lit by one lamp.

collections.name: Where Winter Listens
collections.tagline: For hours held by thin sunlight.
collections.theme: A compact emotional landscape of return, pause, and half-spoken thought.

featuredPoem.title: Training for Final Memory
featuredPoem.firstLines:
Final thought is not random.
It is trained by repetition.

poems.title: What intoxication is this that suddenly gives me hope
The time that my journey takes is long

collections.name: The Stillness Near Departure
collections.theme: A shelf of poems for evenings when ordinary objects feel quietly charged with meaning.

featuredPoem.title: Skill in Action
featuredPoem.firstLines:
Stay even in gain and loss,
in praise and blame,

poems.title: The Love Song of J. Alfred Prufrock

collections.name: A Pocket of Late Sun
collections.tagline: For when streets empty too early.
collections.theme: Poems that stay close to thresholds, departures, and the soft mechanics of return.

featuredPoem.title: La Belle Dame sans Merci
featuredPoem.firstLines:
O what can ail thee, knight-at-arms,
Alone and palely loitering?

poems.title: A narrow fellow in the grass
The light of the sun, the moon, and the stars shines bright
